geographicSummary: District Four covers much of the West Side and Southwest Columbus, including the Hilltop, portions of Franklinton, and areas along West Broad Street. Its northern boundary runs roughly along Route 161 and East Dublin-Granville Road, with I-670 West to the south and Route 3/Cleveland Avenue to the west. The district has the lowest median household income and highest poverty rate among the nine districts.

pointsOfInterest.name: National Veterans Memorial and Museum
pointsOfInterest.description: 53,000-square-foot museum on the Scioto River honoring veterans' experiences with immersive exhibits and a memorial grove.

pointsOfInterest.name: Scioto Mile
pointsOfInterest.description: 175-plus acres of riverfront parks, trails, and fountains along the Scioto River downtown.

pointsOfInterest.name: Camp Chase Confederate Cemetery
pointsOfInterest.description: Historic Civil War cemetery where over 2,000 Confederate prisoners of war are buried.

pointsOfInterest.name: Westgate Community Center
pointsOfInterest.description: Columbus Recreation and Parks community center serving the Westgate neighborhood with programs and facilities.

pointsOfInterest.name: Hilltop Branch Library
pointsOfInterest.description: Columbus Metropolitan Library branch serving the Hilltop neighborhood.

pointsOfInterest.name: Dodge Park
pointsOfInterest.description: Large community park on the West Side with sports fields, playground, and splash pad.

areaCommissions.name: Franklinton Area Commission
areaCommissions.meetingSchedule: Second Tuesday of each month, 6:00 PM
areaCommissions.meetingLocation: Healthy Living Center, 777 W. State St.

data.data_note: Figures from original site launch. Block-group weighted aggregation to council districts.

snapshots.source: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 5-Year via Census API (build_district_profiles.py)

data.data_note: Median income, rent, and home value are population-weighted averages of block-group medians — approximations, not true district medians. See /data/ for methodology.
